regulationSummary.overview: Kentucky has no significant gold deposits and is not a gold prospecting destination. The state's geology is dominated by Ordovician and Devonian limestone in the central Bluegrass region and Pennsylvanian coal-bearing formations in the east and west. The Daniel Boone National Forest covers portions of the Eastern Kentucky coal field and adjacent terrain, but no gold-bearing formations are present. Kentucky is a world-class destination for fossil collecting (marine invertebrates in Ordovician limestone) but not for gold prospectors.
keyRules: Kentucky does not have a recreational gold prospecting permit system — there are no productive gold deposits in the state.
Rock and mineral collecting on Daniel Boone National Forest land is allowed for personal use in reasonable quantities under USFS rules.
In-stream work in Kentucky waterways requires coordination with the Kentucky Division of Water (DOW) and Army Corps of Engineers.
Kentucky state parks prohibit mineral and fossil collecting — focus on National Forest land.
regulationSummary.hpaInfo: Not applicable — there are no gold-bearing streams in Kentucky suitable for prospecting.
regulationSummary.suctionDredgeStatus: Not applicable — Kentucky has no productive placer gold streams.
regulationSummary.handPanning: Gold panning in Kentucky streams is not a viable activity. The state has no gold-bearing geology. The Daniel Boone National Forest is an excellent destination for fossil collecting in Mississippian limestone and sandstone, but not gold prospecting.
regulationSummary.disclaimer: Regulations change. Verify current rules at the official sources listed below before going out.

officialResources.title: Kentucky Geological Survey
officialResources.description: State geological survey — Kentucky has no gold-bearing geology; the survey covers coal, oil, gas, and limestone.

officialResources.title: Daniel Boone National Forest
officialResources.description: USFS-managed land in eastern Kentucky — allows recreational collecting for personal use. Good for fossils, not gold.

officialResources.title: Kentucky Division of Water
officialResources.description: Oversees Kentucky waterway permits and regulations for in-stream work.
